title: Robust Fluffy Tunisian Chicken Tagine
description: A salty and fluffy post-workout Tunisian dish, this recipe features sake-cooking and an ice-bath shock, making it paleo-friendly and delicious.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together sake, olive oil, onion, garlic, ginger, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chicken thighs to the bowl and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the marinade and place it on a slate tile.
Bake the chicken in the preheated oven for 25 minutes.
While the chicken is baking, prepare an ice bath by filling a large bowl with ice and water.
After the chicken has baked for 25 minutes, remove it from the oven and immediately submerge it in the ice bath to stop the cooking process.
Let the chicken rest in the ice bath for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
